title: China Is Not Europe’s Competitiveness Bogeyman
content: Calls for European leaders to protect domestic industry from "unfairly" subsidized Chinese competition have lately grown louder. But while tariffs might offer temporary relief to a few sectors, they cannot restore Europe's technological leadership, industrial dynamism, or export competitiveness.

creator: Daniel Gros
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Putting American Science on a MAGA Leash
content: A draft rule from US President Donald Trump’s White House Office of Management and Budget would subject all scientific research funding to the whims of political appointees, further undercutting a pillar of American power. The goal is to foreclose any future that is plural, contestable, and beyond the president’s control.

creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Measles Came Roaring Back
content: Bangladesh is facing soaring measles cases not because of a single localized failure, but because of years of systemic erosion. Ultimately, maintaining herd immunity against highly contagious viruses requires relentless attention to supply and demand, as well as to the broader social and institutional foundations of public health.
snippet: Bangladesh is facing soaring measles cases not because of a single localized failure, but because of years of systemic erosion. Ultimately, maintaining herd immunity against highly contagious viruses
creator: M. Niaz Asadullah
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Pete Hegseth’s Dangerous Call to Arms in Asia
content: Much like it did in Europe, the Trump administration has called on US allies in the Indo-Pacific to increase military spending to 3.5% of GDP. But a defense buildup on this scale would jeopardize economic growth and regional unity, and could lead Southeast Asian countries to gravitate toward China’s strategic orbit.
snippet: Much like it did in Europe, the Trump administration has called on US allies in the Indo-Pacific to increase military spending to 3.5% of GDP. But a defense buildup on this scale would jeopardize
creator: Thitinan Pongsudhirak
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Pope’s AI Vision and Its Limits
content: Pope Leo XIV’s encyclical on AI is clear-eyed about the limits of techno-utopianism. But its vision of technology directed toward human flourishing needs to be extended to include the interests of every sentient being, human and nonhuman, biological or—possibly—artificial.
snippet: Pope Leo XIV’s encyclical on AI is clear-eyed about the limits of techno-utopianism. But its vision of technology directed toward human flourishing needs to be extended to include the interests of
creator: Peter Singer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Democracy vs. Data Centers
content: As tech companies race to build data centers, communities and lawmakers across the United States are pushing back. Industry leaders frame local opposition as a threat to American competitiveness, but China’s experience shows that unchecked expansion carries serious risks of its own.
snippet: As tech companies race to build data centers, communities and lawmakers across the United States are pushing back. Industry leaders frame local opposition as a threat to American competitiveness, but
creator: Angela Huyue Zhang
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Right Incentives for Climate Action
content: As the closure of the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt global oil and gas flows, countries are increasingly focusing on short-term energy priorities to the detriment of long-term decarbonization goals. In such a context, the difficult task of designing climate policies that realign incentives takes on new importance.
snippet: As the closure of the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt global oil and gas flows, countries are increasingly focusing on short-term energy priorities to the detriment of long-term decarbonization
creator: Paula Carvalho Pereda
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The US and India Have Become Regional Rivals
content: While the US views India as a counterweight to China in Asia, it balks at the idea of Indian regional dominance, preferring a pluralistic order. The Trump administration has gone further by pursuing close ties with Pakistan, apparently hoping that the US and India can remain global partners.
snippet: While the US views India as a counterweight to China in Asia, it balks at the idea of Indian regional dominance, preferring a pluralistic order. The Trump administration has gone further by pursuing
creator: Brahma Chellaney
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Digital Sovereignty Is in the Fine Print
content: Despite efforts to build their own digital industries and infrastructure, most countries remain dependent on American tech firms for the provision of core government services. But government officials can tip the balance of bargaining power by attaching certain standardized provisions to public procurement and services contracts.
snippet: Despite efforts to build their own digital industries and infrastructure, most countries remain dependent on American tech firms for the provision of core government services. But government officials
creator: Gabriela Ramos
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump Wants to Privatize His Presidential Records
content: By enacting the Presidential Records Act in response to Richard Nixon’s malfeasance, the US Congress created a process to limit abuse of power and ensure accountability. Like Nixon, Donald Trump seems determined to avoid accountability, which has led his Justice Department to argue—poorly—that the PRA is unconstitutional.
snippet: By enacting the Presidential Records Act in response to Richard Nixon’s malfeasance, the US Congress created a process to limit abuse of power and ensure accountability. Like Nixon, Donald Trump seems
creator: Mark J. Rozell
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: British Steel Needs a Transformation, Not a Bailout
content: The recent revival of industrial policy offers an opportunity to break the old pattern whereby economic risks are socialized while rewards remain privatized. In addition to attaching meaningful conditionalities to public support, governments must invest in the state capacity required to govern strategic sectors well.
snippet: The recent revival of industrial policy offers an opportunity to break the old pattern whereby economic risks are socialized while rewards remain privatized. In addition to attaching meaningful
creator: Mariana Mazzucato
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How to Encourage Deceased Organ Donation
content: Developing countries facing organ shortages often search for complex solutions, such as sweeping legal reforms. But Tunisia’s experience suggests a much simpler first step: awareness-raising sessions where doctors explain how the transplant system works, and people share their fears and concerns.
snippet: Developing countries facing organ shortages often search for complex solutions, such as sweeping legal reforms. But Tunisia’s experience suggests a much simpler first step: awareness-raising sessions
creator: Christina Sarah Hauser
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Building Alliances of America’s Allies
content: In a world in which the US cannot be counted on as it once was, the objective is not stability at any price, but rather stability on terms consistent with national and Western interests. This is achievable, but only if the friends of the US recognize the new reality and act individually and collectively to meet the challenge.
snippet: In a world in which the US cannot be counted on as it once was, the objective is not stability at any price, but rather stability on terms consistent with national and Western interests. This is
creator: Richard Haass
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Promise and Limits of African Data Sovereignty
content: African countries are recognizing that no matter how well-designed their data-protection regulations are, their sovereignty over US-based Big Tech firms ends at the boundary between subsidiary and parent. Overcoming this barrier will require a level of coordination that the continent has yet to achieve.
snippet: African countries are recognizing that no matter how well-designed their data-protection regulations are, their sovereignty over US-based Big Tech firms ends at the boundary between subsidiary and
creator: Samuel W. Ugwumba
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The AI Economy’s Permanent Underclass
content: AI is transforming the global economy while widening the gap between technological winners and losers. Governments that fail to secure a place in the AI supply chain may find themselves confronting mass job displacement without the tax revenues or state capacity needed to contain the social and political fallout.
snippet: AI is transforming the global economy while widening the gap between technological winners and losers. Governments that fail to secure a place in the AI supply chain may find themselves confronting
creator: Kenneth Rogoff
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The One Man Who Could Give Trump Middle East Peace
content: With a large majority of UN member states recognizing Palestine on the 1967 lines, there is a clear international blueprint for creating an independent Palestinian state alongside Israel. What has been missing is credible Palestinian leadership of the kind that the imprisoned politician Marwan Barghouti could provide.
snippet: With a large majority of UN member states recognizing Palestine on the 1967 lines, there is a clear international blueprint for creating an independent Palestinian state alongside Israel. What has
creator: Daoud Kuttab
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europe’s Nuclear Options
content: The risks raised by nuclear weapons remain as potent as ever, but the institutional framework that mitigated them has been severely eroded. The task now is to prevent deterrence from giving way to proliferation—which, for Europe, means building conventional capabilities, keeping the US engaged, and upholding restraint.
snippet: The risks raised by nuclear weapons remain as potent as ever, but the institutional framework that mitigated them has been severely eroded. The task now is to prevent deterrence from giving way to
creator: Ana Palacio
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Intelligence That AI Is Missing
content: For AI to simplify humans’ complex social and professional lives, it cannot just give tidy answers based on whatever the internet has to say about our values. It will need the capacity to account for our relational commitments, which are typically grounded in personal identity, experience, and culture.
snippet: For AI to simplify humans’ complex social and professional lives, it cannot just give tidy answers based on whatever the internet has to say about our values. It will need the capacity to account for
creator: Anne-Marie Slaughter
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Isn’t Europe Poorer Than the US?
content: Nobel laureate Paul Krugman has a point when he says that European living standards have largely kept pace with those in the US, despite flagging growth and innovation. But this will not remain true indefinitely: restoring Europe's ability to compete at the technological frontier remains essential to its future well-being.
snippet: Nobel laureate Paul Krugman has a point when he says that European living standards have largely kept pace with those in the US, despite flagging growth and innovation. But this will not remain true
creator: Dalia Marin
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Real Choice Confronting Developing Countries
content: Lost in the debate about whether developing countries should pursue manufacturing- or services-led growth is the potential for new AI tools to make even informal workers far more productive. If governments can broaden access to such technologies, they no longer need to choose between strategies.
snippet: Lost in the debate about whether developing countries should pursue manufacturing- or services-led growth is the potential for new AI tools to make even informal workers far more productive. If
creator: Ravi Venkatesan
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: India’s Great Political Realignment
content: In recent years, the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party’s focus on religious identity, together with its “purification” of voter rolls, has created a more binary—and potentially volatile—political landscape, where elections increasingly reflect ethno-religious identity. This month’s elections in five states largely confirmed this trend.

creator: Shashi Tharoor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Central Banking in an Age of Global Supply Shocks
content: The core assumptions underlying the decades-old inflation-targeting orthodoxy have proven to be unfit for a geopolitically fragmented world characterized by frequent supply-side disruptions. Under such conditions, central banks' traditional monetary policies are only as effective as markets believe them to be.

creator: Şebnem   Kalemli-Özcan
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Tech-MAGA Breakup Is Coming
content: Donald Trump has managed to fuse a backward-looking populist movement with a Silicon Valley vanguard whose guiding assumption is that AI will render a large share of ordinary people economically redundant. But with the tech billionaires now rewiring America's electricity grid and driving up costs, a split seems inevitable.
snippet: Donald Trump has managed to fuse a backward-looking populist movement with a Silicon Valley vanguard whose guiding assumption is that AI will render a large share of ordinary people economically
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Professor Should Work Hard and Grade Easy
content: The Harvard University faculty has now decided to limit the share of “A” grades given to students to about 20%. But, as the move's defenders freely admit, the intent is to increase professors' power and students' stress, not to generate excitement in the classroom.
snippet: The Harvard University faculty has now decided to limit the share of “A” grades given to students to about 20%. But, as the move's defenders freely admit, the intent is to increase professors' power
creator: James K. Galbraith
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Iran War Is Fueling a Global Debt Shock
content: Once again, a global crisis is disproportionately burdening countries that did not cause it. Across the Global South, the food- and energy-price shock triggered by the conflict in the Middle East has morphed into a debt shock that demands an urgent international response.
snippet: Once again, a global crisis is disproportionately burdening countries that did not cause it. Across the Global South, the food- and energy-price shock triggered by the conflict in the Middle East has
creator: Moussa Faki Mahamat
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Rape of Venezuela
content: Venezuela’s oil production is rising, yet its currency is depreciating, inflation is accelerating, and foreign exchange remains scarce. The disconnect reflects a political economy built on private dealmaking and a mutually beneficial alliance between ruling elites and US President Donald Trump’s acolytes.

creator: Ricardo Hausmann
